profile_description: RESPONSIBILITIES: As part of our strong growth, we are looking for a GNSS Navigation Algorithms Engineer to join our teams. Directly reporting to the Inertial Navigation Algorithms Manager, you will join our R&D teams to participate in the design of GNSS signal processing algorithms. In direct contact with other mathematical and software development engineers as well as the project manager, you will be part of the Inertial Navigation research team. You will join a team of 3 people. The topics covered will be linked to inertial navigation, GNSS processing, and the in-depth study of the characteristics of these signals in difficult environments such as jamming, spoofing or multi-paths in order to optimize system performance. In particular, you will work on RAIM algorithms, as well as the study and modeling of measurement noise. Your mathematical knowledge will allow you to understand, implement and validate complex algorithms. You will also participate in the definition of the software architecture as well as in the testing phases and the production of associated documentation. You will have the opportunity to use many technologies (Kalman Filtering, Least Squares, FI and IIR). Your main missions: • Understanding of complex algorithms such as GNSS RTK, PPP and INS • Participation in the definition of the most appropriate software architecture • Implementation in C++ in a real-time context • Optimization of algorithm settings (Kalman filtering, RAIM) • Participation in project planning: estimation of deadlines and tasks necessary for the mission • Validation of results and performance analysis on Matlab or Python • Documentation This list is not limiting. PROFILE WANTED: • With a BAC + 5 education with a specialization in applied mathematics, embedded systems or information systems and you can demonstrate solid mathematical/algorithmic skills. • You have experience (> 4 years) in the field of inertial navigation or GPS/GNSS, or another field using similar techniques. • You are proficient in C++, Matlab and/or Python languages. • Kalman filtering, least squares or other data fusion algorithms have no secrets for you? • Are you interested in MEMS (or FOG, RLG), sensors, GPS/GNSS, signal processing (FIR, IIR) technologies? Other desirable skills: • Use of source code control such as GIT, SubVersion. • Use of project management tools such as Confluence and Jira. Recruitment process: At SBG Systems the recruitment process is simple and quick! • 1 telephone or video exchange with an HR representative • 1 technical test • 1 face-to-face exchange with the Manager (meeting the team and visiting the premises at the same time) Guaranteed response! *As part of this position, a defense security clearance will be required. Range: between €48,000 and €53,000/year. • Advantages: Participation Bonus (PEE - PERECO), Restaurant Ticket Title, 2 days of teleworking / week... profile_description: RESPONSIBILITIES: Our client, a recognized player in the design and production of electrical, mechanical and automated systems, supports its industrial clients on tailor-made projects of high technical value. Join a passionate team, at the heart of a stimulating technical environment where projects are varied and discussions are rich! AUTOMATICIAN (M/F) Permanent contract – PESSAC (33) – €33K / €40K Reporting to the Project Manager, you participate in the design and development of automated solutions, in close collaboration with the mechanical and electrical teams. Your missions: Analyze & design • Study customer needs and define the functional and technical architecture of the solution. • Design and program the automation of industrial machines and equipment. • Select the appropriate automation components and ensure their integration. Develop & commission • Carry out programming of PLCs (Siemens, Schneider, etc.) and human-machine interfaces (HMI). • Perform testing, validation and commissioning on site. • Participate in the development of automated systems and customer tests. Document & collaborate • Write technical documentation (manuals, machine files). • Work in synergy with the mechanical, electrical and production departments. PROFILE WANTED: With Bac +2 to Bac +3 training (BTS Automation, DUT Electrical Engineering and Industrial IT or equivalent), you have a first successful experience in industrial automation, ideally on special machines or tailor-made equipment. You are proficient in automaton programming and the design of automated systems. Knowledge of Siemens, Schneider or Rockwell environments is a plus. Curious, rigorous and equipped with a good analytical mind, you enjoy taking on technical challenges and working in a team on innovative projects. Contractual information and benefits • Permanent contract – Technician status • Remuneration over 13 months • Christmas check • Participation bonus Recruitment process NEXTGEN RH, a multi-specialist headhunting firm, supports you throughout the process through a fundamentally human approach: • Telephone exchange with your recruitment manager • In-depth interview with your recruitment manager • Interviews with our client
Mapped Skills: ['align software with system architectures', 'laboratory equipment', 'manufacture of electrical wire products', 'programmable logic controller', 'assess customers', 'recruit personnel', 'collect customer feedback on applications', 'automatic control system', 'human-computer interaction', 'design automation components']
